title: Getting started with flexstanr
text: root Stan package, run setup helper : adds flexstanr (rstan, default backend) Imports , flexstanr still pre-CRAN, interim Remotes: ACCIDDA/flexstanr entry remotes / pak can install GitHub. flexstanr CRAN, pass on_cran = TRUE skip Remotes entry.

title: Getting started with flexstanr
text: stan_options() collects validates sampler arguments chosen backend, forwarding verbatim call feels native backend: backend argument vocabulary, mixing caught early “mean” hint rather failing deep inside sampler:
code: opts <- stan_options(chains = 2, iter = 500, seed = 1) str(opts) #> List of 4 #>  $ iter   : int 500 #>  $ seed   : int 1 #>  $ chains : int 2 #>  $ backend: chr "rstan" # `parallel_chains` is a cmdstanr word; the rstan backend rejects it. try(stan_options(backend = "rstan", parallel_chains = 4)) #> Error : These stan_options() arguments are not valid for the 'rstan' backend: #>   - `parallel_chains`: use `cores`

title: Getting started with flexstanr
text: fit_model() dispatches backend recorded options resolves compiled model name package. host fitting one models needs extra arguments; calling package detected automatically.
code: # `"coverage"` is resolved from your package's stanmodels (rstan) or # inst/stan/coverage.stan (cmdstanr). fit <- fit_model(   "coverage",   dat_stan  = data_list,   init      = init_list,   stan_opts = opts )

title: Getting started with flexstanr
text: backend_* accessors read fitted object without code needing know backend produced : Unrecognized objects pass backend_has_draws() carry draws, test doubles left untouched:
code: # posterior draws as an iterations x chains x parameters array draws <- backend_draws_array(fit)  # named parameters, matching rstan::extract()'s shape post <- backend_extract(fit, pars = c("beta", "sigma"))  # guard against the degenerate "no draws" case before using a fit stopifnot(backend_has_draws(fit)) backend_has_draws(list()) #> [1] TRUE

title: Getting started with flexstanr
text: Pass backend = "cmdstanr" stan_options(). cmdstanr optional CRAN, install separately (see cmdstanr getting-started guide); selecting without package installed errors early actionable message.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Carl Pearson. Author, maintainer. Weston Voglesonger. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Pearson C, Voglesonger W (2026). flexstanr: Portable Backend Layer 'Stan' Models. R package version 0.1.0, https://accidda.github.io/flexstanr/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {flexstanr: Portable Backend Layer for 'Stan' Models},   author = {Carl Pearson and Weston Voglesonger},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://accidda.github.io/flexstanr/}, }

what: Portable Backend Layer for Stan Models
title: Portable Backend Layer for Stan Models
text: portable Stan-backend layer R packages fit Stan model. gives host package one interface fitting either rstan (optionally) cmdstanr, code works whichever backend installed. flexstanr compiles Stan : host package supplies compiled models, flexstanr resolves calling package run time.

title: Portable Backend Layer for Stan Models
text: stan_options() collects validates sampler arguments chosen backend, forwarding verbatim calls feel native. Mixing one backend’s argument vocabulary errors “mean” hint. fit_model() dispatches fit backend recorded options, resolving compiled model name calling package. fit-consumption accessors read fit backend-agnostically: backend_draws_array(), backend_extract(), backend_generate_quantities(), backend_has_draws().

title: Portable Backend Layer for Stan Models
text: flexstanr yet CRAN. Install development version GitHub: default backend, rstan, hard dependency. cmdstanr optional backend; CRAN, project wants installs separately (see cmdstanr getting-started guide).

title: Portable Backend Layer for Stan Models
text: Declare flexstanr dependency call fitting code: model name resolved package’s compiled models (stanmodels rstan, inst/stan/<name>.stan cmdstanr); calling package detected automatically. Run flexstanr::use_flexstanr() package root wire dependencies DESCRIPTION. Note. flexstanr began use_standalone() script vendored ACCIDDA Stan packages (imuGAP, hestia, SeverityEstimate). now proper package consumers import rather copy. See flexstanr 0.1.0 CRAN release milestone.
code: # in your package opts <- flexstanr::stan_options(chains = 4, iter = 2000, seed = 1) fit  <- flexstanr::fit_model(   "coverage",              # resolved from your package's stanmodels / inst/stan   dat_stan  = data_list,   init      = init_list,   stan_opts = opts ) draws <- flexstanr::backend_draws_array(fit)

title: Run generated quantities against a fit and return a parameter matrix — backend_generate_quantities
text: raw_fit backend-native fit object (rstan stanfit cmdstanr CmdStanMCMC). data Stan data list generated-quantities run. draws_mat draws matrix (rows = draws, columns = parameters). Used rstan backend; cmdstanr backend runs generated quantities fit's draws ignores argument. pars name generated parameter return. model_name name model whose generated-quantities block run. Required cmdstanr backend, recompiles model run ; ignored rstan, reuses model carried raw_fit. package host package model belongs ; defaults calling package (see fit_model()). used cmdstanr backend.

what: Does a fit object carry usable posterior draws? — backend_has_draws
title: Does a fit object carry usable posterior draws? — backend_has_draws
text: Detect degenerate "draws" case fit, caller can fail loudly instead returning empty fit. backend-aware: rstan returns mode-2 stanfit empty @sim sampler fails initialize (rather erroring), cmdstanr exposes draws $draws(). Unrecognized objects (e.g. test mocks) treated draws pass untouched.

title: Does a fit object carry usable posterior draws? — backend_has_draws
Does a fit object carry usable posterior draws? — backend_has_draws
text: raw_fit backend-native fit object (rstan stanfit cmdstanr CmdStanMCMC).

title: Does a fit object carry usable posterior draws? — backend_has_draws
text: logical; TRUE fit carries usable draws.

title: Does a fit object carry usable posterior draws? — backend_has_draws
code: # Unrecognized objects are treated as carrying draws (pass-through). backend_has_draws(list()) #> [1] TRUE

what: flexstanr: Portable Backend Layer for 'Stan' Models — flexstanr-package
title: flexstanr: Portable Backend Layer for 'Stan' Models — flexstanr-package
text: Gives 'Stan'-based R package one interface fitting models either 'rstan' (optionally) 'cmdstanr'. Collects validates sampler options, guarding mixing one backend's argument vocabulary , dispatches fit chosen backend, exposes backend-agnostic accessors reading posterior draws, extracting parameters, running generated quantities. host package supplies compiled models; flexstanr resolves calling package run time, code works whichever backend installed.

title: flexstanr: Portable Backend Layer for 'Stan' Models — flexstanr-package
text: Maintainer: Carl Pearson carl.ab.pearson@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Carl Pearson carl.ab.pearson@gmail.com (ORCID) Weston Voglesonger westonvogle@gmail.com

title: Stan Sampler Options — stan_options
text: Collects validates sampler arguments chosen backend, forwarding verbatim calls feel native backend. Use backend's argument names; mixing one backend's vocabulary errors hint. model object supplied separately (via fit_model()), data init constructed internally, none may set . chains defaults 4 downstream code can always size per-chain structures .

title: Stan Sampler Options — stan_options
Stan Sampler Options — stan_options
text: ... sampler arguments forwarded verbatim chosen backend's sampler. Use backend's names: "rstan", rstan::sampling() arguments (iter, cores, seed); "cmdstanr", $sample() arguments (iter_warmup, iter_sampling, parallel_chains, ...). chains positive integer specifying number Markov chains.     default 4. backend Stan interface target, one "rstan" (default) "cmdstanr". Determines argument vocabulary accepted sampler fit_model() calls. Selecting "cmdstanr" errors cmdstanr package installed.

title: Stan Sampler Options — stan_options
text: named list validated sampler arguments, carrying backend element recording backend built

title: Stan Sampler Options — stan_options
code: stan_options() #> $chains #> [1] 4 #>  #> $backend #> [1] "rstan" #>  stan_options(chains = 2, iter = 500) #> $iter #> [1] 500 #>  #> $chains #> [1] 2 #>  #> $backend #> [1] "rstan" #>  if (requireNamespace("cmdstanr", quietly = TRUE)) {   stan_options(backend = "cmdstanr", parallel_chains = 4, iter_warmup = 500) } #> $parallel_chains #> [1] 4 #>  #> $iter_warmup #> [1] 500 #>  #> $chains #> [1] 4 #>  #> $backend #> [1] "cmdstanr" #>

what: Wire flexstanr into a host package — use_flexstanr
title: Wire flexstanr into a host package — use_flexstanr
text: one-time setup helper, spirit usethis's use_* functions, declares flexstanr dependency host package run . adds flexstanr (rstan, default backend) host's Imports , flexstanr CRAN, interim Remotes entry remotes / pak can install GitHub. wiring needed: host calls fit_model() backend_* accessors directly, flexstanr resolves host's compiled models automatically calling package.

title: Wire flexstanr into a host package — use_flexstanr
Wire flexstanr into a host package — use_flexstanr
text: path path host package's root (directory containing DESCRIPTION). Defaults working directory. remote GitHub owner/repo used interim Remotes entry flexstanr pre-CRAN. on_cran set TRUE flexstanr CRAN skip interim Remotes entry; plain Imports dependency enough.

title: Wire flexstanr into a host package — use_flexstanr
text: host package path, invisibly.

title: Wire flexstanr into a host package — use_flexstanr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # from the root of your Stan package: flexstanr::use_flexstanr() } # }

title: flexstanr 0.1.0
text: Initial release: portable Stan-backend layer Stan-based R package can fit models , using either rstan (default) , optionally, cmdstanr. stan_options() collects validates sampler options chosen backend, forwarding verbatim guarding mixing one backend’s argument vocabulary . fit_model() dispatches fit backend recorded options resolves calling package’s compiled model automatically. Backend-agnostic accessors read fit without knowing backend produced : backend_draws_array(), backend_extract(), backend_generate_quantities(), backend_has_draws(). use_flexstanr() wires flexstanr host package’s DESCRIPTION. cmdstanr optional backend, used requireNamespace() guards; rstan default hard Stan dependency.
